title: Executive Order 10956—Amendment of Executive Order No. 10841, Relating to International Cooperation Under the Atomic Energy Act of 1954, as Amended

flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment adding a review layer to an existing determination process.
The amendment adds an explicit presidential-referral-and-disapproval step for determinations that previously lacked one.
A procedural amendment, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing procedural amendment to an ongoing international-cooperation framework.

This adds a new presidential review-and-disapproval requirement rather than removing any existing check — the mirror image of the quiet-regression pattern identified elsewhere in this project (EO 12072, EO 9296).
No compliance regime is created by this order.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Notable as a 'quiet strengthening': the amendment adds a presidential referral-and-disapproval requirement to atomic-energy international-cooperation determinations that previously had no such check, the mirror image of the quiet-regression pattern already logged for EO 12072 and EO 9296.
